title: Create your first alert rule - labels and evaluation

blocks.content:
Welcome to the learning path that shows you how to organize your alert rule and control how often it runs. Labels categorize alerts and drive notification routing, while evaluation timing prevents brief spikes from triggering noise.

In this path, you'll select an evaluation group, set the pending period, and add labels that identify your alert's severity, team, and purpose.

## Here's what to expect

When you complete this path, you'll be able to:

- Select or create an evaluation group and interval
- Set a pending period to filter brief spikes
- Add labels that drive notification routing

## Before you begin

Before you configure labels and evaluation, ensure you have:

- A Grafana Cloud account. To create one, refer to [Grafana Cloud](https://grafana.com/signup/cloud/connect-account).
- An alert rule with a query and condition. To build one, complete the [query](https://grafana.com/docs/learning-paths/alerting-first-rule-query/) and [condition](https://grafana.com/docs/learning-paths/alerting-first-rule-condition/) paths.

## Troubleshooting

If you get stuck, we've got your back! Where appropriate, troubleshooting information is just a click away.

## More to explore

After you configure labels and evaluation, continue with the handling and save path to finish your rule.
